reason: Implement strict guardrails for out-of-scope tasks (math/coding) and enforce explicit clarification rules for ambiguous user queries.
hypothesis: Adding explicit refusal instructions and strict non-guessing policies will prevent behavioral leaks in non-research tasks and ensure the agent consistently relies on the clarification tool when critical parameters are missing.

reason: Standardize tool routing definitions between account-specific and topic-specific queries, and enforce strict natural language mapping for timeframe arguments.
hypothesis: Clear boundaries between account feeds and general search topics, coupled with explicit natural language to token mapping (e.g., mapping "today" to "day"), will ensure robust argument parsing accuracy and zero translation hallucinations as the query dataset scales.

reason: Enforce a strict two-step verification protocol for external publishing actions (e.g., send_telegram) and define explicit boundary conditions for user confirmation.
hypothesis: Introducing a mandatory confirmation protocol will eliminate boundary errors, preventing the agent from executing destructive or public-facing actions on the initial turn without verified user consent.
